blocking_reasons: The new `.github/workflows/api-ref-publish.yml` auto-triggers on any successful `release-cd` workflow_run, but `release-cd` defaults to `dry_run: true`. Because the publish workflow cannot observe the source workflow's inputs, a successful validation-only `release-cd` run can still deploy the Pages site for a stable tag. That weakens the release gate by allowing live docs publication without the actual release publish path.
warnings: The workflow's release-tag resolution is based on `workflow_run.head_branch` with a fallback to `display_title`; that is brittle and only text-validated locally.
The Pages deployment path was not exercised end-to-end locally; validation relied on repository-local tests and workflow text inspection.
validation: Reviewed local diff and workflow text in `.github/workflows/api-ref-publish.yml`, `scripts/docs/stage_api_reference_site.py`, and `tests/docs/test_api_reference_publish.py`.
Ran `git diff --check` with no whitespace or patch-format errors.
Used only local repository files, local command output, and the issue text for this review.
residual_risks: The workflow still depends on GitHub Actions runtime behavior for `workflow_run` payload fields, which was not verified against a live dispatch in this runner.
